task_objective: Fix SteamOS games dropdown long-name regression with a narrow, temporary frontend workaround, avoiding broad wildcard selector side effects.
design_decisions: Wrap selected dropdown option value in a span with class sdh-ludusavi-game-dropdown-value using DropdownItem's renderButtonValue prop.

Replace old broad styles in dropdownStyleEl with targeted, scoped rules that apply max-width: 100% and min-width: 0 to the dropdown elements only (using class prefixes and div tags, avoiding the '*' wildcard).
Add non-collapsing rules (flex-shrink: 0, min-width: fit-content) for svg, chevron, icon, and arrow child components within the dropdown to protect them from collapse.
Add explicit code comments marking this styling workaround as temporary and explaining how to roll it back.
results: Static frontend validation, Ruff linting/formatting, Python type checks, Pytest, TypeScript compilation, and Rollup build all pass successfully.

review_subagent_evidence.findings: No blocking issues found. The implementation matches the saved plan perfectly and satisfies all repository protocol requirements. Verified wildcard removal, dropdown layout, and custom CSS properties.
residual_risks: Class Prefix Dependency: The workaround overrides CSS based on class prefixes like [class*='DropdownField' i]. If Decky or the underlying SteamOS client updates internal component classes, these styles will not target those components, which could cause a regression.
test_gaps: No Visual/Layout Testing: The static tests only test codebase string matching and regex logic. They cannot test computed styles, actual element widths, or layout boundaries under SteamOS.
